message:
chore: gitignore beads runtime metadata + swarm coordination scratch

aadc's .gitignore only covered `.bv/`, Rust build output, and a short
ephemeral-agent-artifact list, so everyday per-session debris kept
landing in `git status`:

- `.beads/.br_history/` — beads CLI rotates a timestamped issues.jsonl
  snapshot on every sync, accumulating large per-second files that
  never belong in history.
- `.beads/last-touched`, `.beads/.bv.lock`, `.beads/*.lock` — the
  advisory write/sync flocks and the "touched by" marker that the
  beads + bv stack writes on every invocation.
- `.ntm/` — NTM agent pane working tree (logs + session state).
- `.swarm/` and `.swarm_marching_orders.md` — multi-agent swarm
  orchestrator scratch files (ticks.log, orchestrator.md,
  review_orders.txt, phase.txt, marching orders).

These match the canonical patterns already present in beads_rust,
remote_compilation_helper, and frankensqlite; aligning aadc keeps
`ru status` reliably clean across the fleet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,17 @@
 # bv (beads viewer) local config and caches
 .bv/
 
+# Beads ephemeral runtime files (history snapshots, advisory locks, transient metadata)
+.beads/.br_history/
+.beads/last-touched
+.beads/.bv.lock
+.beads/*.lock
+
+# Multi-agent coordination scratch state
+.ntm/
+.swarm/
+.swarm_marching_orders.md
+
 # Ephemeral/temporary files (agent workflow artifacts)
 RESEARCH_FINDINGS.md
 TOON_INTEGRATION_BRIEF.md
